demos.report: EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST   CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMPARISON:  None.    TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast head CT performed using radiation dose reduction techniques.  Technical factors are evaluated and adjusted to ensure appropriate moderation of exposure.  Automated dose management technology is applied to adjust radiation exposure  while achieving a diagnostic quality image.    FINDINGS:    There is evidence of acute right MCA territorial infarction with loss of cortical gray-white matter differentiation of the mid lateral right precentral gyrus (series 303, image 45). No evidence of hemorrhagic transformation or mass effect.    There is small chronic infarction involving the right superior cerebellar hemisphere. There are Virchow-Robin perivascular spaces versus chronic lacunar infarctions in bilateral basal ganglia. There are mild nonspecific patchy areas of hypoattenuation in  periventricular, deep, and subcortical white matter, probably from chronic ischemic small vessel disease.    Ventricles are age-appropriate in size and configuration. No extra-axial fluid collection.    Visualized paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are clear. Bones, orbits, and soft tissues are unremarkable.    IMPRESSION:    1. Evidence of acute right MCA territorial infarction involving right precentral gyrus. No hemorrhagic transformation.    2. Small chronic right cerebellar infarction and mild chronic ischemic small vessel disease changes.    Findings were discussed with Dr. Bhavsar on 2/4/2022 5:49 PM, and she verbalized understanding of the report.    HMH-2UA70310L8

demos.report: EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST   COMPARISON:  August 21, 2020.    CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected    COMMENTS:  Axial noncontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Sagittal and coronal reconstructions were obtained.    CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose.        FINDINGS:  There is subtle cortical and subcortical low density at the left parietal lobe which is a progressive finding compared to the previous study.    There is mild mucosal thickening in the visualized sinuses. There is calcific atherosclerotic change in the arteries at the skull base.    IMPRESSION:  No acute intracranial hemorrhage. Possible recent ischemic change at the left occipital lobe. Correlation with MRI is recommended.    The findings were discussed with Dr. Noffsinger who verbalized understanding with read back June 6, 2022 at 7:25 AM.      1RM1RAD_PS02

demos.report: EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control.  IMPRESSION:  Large occlusive hypodense clot in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with acute infarction in the left basal ganglia and left corona radiata with is elevation of the cortical gray matter and an ASPECT score of approximately 8. There is no acute hemorrhage or mass effect. There is no midline shift. The ventricles are normal in size. The basal cisterns are patent. The calvarium is unremarkable. Findings discussed with  Shokraneh-Kenari, Keyon, MD on 12/18/2024 7:52 PM , and he verbalized understanding of the report. 1RM1RADPS82

demos.report: EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMPARISON:  None TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast CT of the brain was performed. Both soft tissue and bone reconstruction algorithms are interpreted. CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction techniques and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. FINDINGS: There is an acute infarct involving the entire left MCA and ACA territories (Aspects 0). There is mild local mass effect with some narrowing of the sulci. There is no midline shift. No hemorrhage is seen. The left carotid terminus and proximal left MCA M1 segment hyperdense suggesting thrombus. Moderate to extensive chronic small vessel ischemic changes are noted in the right cerebral white matter. Moderate right-sided involutional changes of brain are present. Chronic lacunar infarct is noted in the left cerebellar hemisphere. Visualized portions of the paranasal sinuses show no air-fluid level. Mastoid air cells are clear. No fracture or aggressive bony lesion is seen.   IMPRESSION:  Acute infarct involving the entire left MCA and ACA territories. The left carotid terminus and proximal left MCA M1 segment are hyperdense suggesting thrombus. Findings were discussed with Dr. EMILY LEEDE at 12/20/2024 6:34 PM who verbalized understanding. 1RM1RAD_PS46

signature.instructions:
Label acute/recent stroke territory from radiology text.

/no_think

Return final answer only. Do not include hidden reasoning, scratchpad,
chain-of-thought, analysis, self-correction, or explanation.

Return only allowed labels. Use NONE only when the report has no
qualifying acute/recent/current territory evidence. Prefer the smaller
label set when evidence is ambiguous, and never include NONE with a real
territory label.
